title: Conjecture 1.2 (Dvořák–Král–Nejedlý–Škrekovski)
statement_text: There exists $\Delta_0$ such that if $G$ is a planar graph with girth at least 5 and $\Delta(G) \geq \Delta_0$ then $\chi(G^2) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$.
context_text: Dvořák, Král, Nejedlý, and Škrekovski conjectured this in 2008 as an analogue of their girth-6 result, where they showed $\chi(G^2) \leq \Delta + 2$ for planar graphs of girth at least 6 with $\Delta \geq 8821$. The conjecture posits that a similar bound holds one girth level lower, at girth at least 5.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full and strengthens it to list-coloring and paintability: every planar graph of girth at least 5 with $\Delta(G) \geq \Delta_0 = 2{,}992{,}901$ is square $(\Delta(G)+2)$-choosable and square $(\Delta(G)+2)$-paintable.
paper_title: Planar Graphs of Girth at least Five are Square $(Δ+ 2)$-Choosable
